original: Ann Hodges is bruised by a meteor at Sylacauga, Alabama, in the first modern instance of a meteorite striking a human
World's longest recorded rainbow lasts for 8 hours and 58 minutes in Taipei's Yangmingshan mountain range
Artificial intelligence Chatbot ChatGPT prototype released by A.I. lab OpenAI, able to answer complex questions, write poetry and mimic some human emotions
Ottumwa, Iowa, declared "Video Game Capital of the World" by mayor Jerry Parker
Newly discovered rock art announced found in the Serranía La Lindosa, Colombian Amazon, dated 12,600 and 11,800 years ago with thousands of paintings of now extinct Ice Age animals
ArchaeologistHeinrich Schliemanndiscovers the gold Mask of Agamemnon at Mycenae in modern Greece, known as "the Mona Lisa of prehistory"
"Thriller," the sixth studio album byMichael Jacksonis released (Grammy Award Album of the Year 1984, best-selling album of all time, Billboard Album of the Year 1983)
Joan Ganz Cooney, American educational television writer and producer (co-founder of the Children's Television Workshop; Sesame Street), born in Phoenix, Arizona
Benjamin Franklinreceives the Godfrey Copley medal "on account of his curious Experiments and Observations on Electricity"
Alphafold's DeepMind AI program announces it has achieved scientific breakthrough by being able to predict how proteins fold into 3D shapes
Josephine Bakerbecomes the first black woman to be honored at Paris’ Panthéon, France's highest honor

At 21 years, 10 months, 3 weeks, 5 daysFloyd Pattersonbecomes youngest world heavyweight boxing champion; KOsArchie Moorein 5th round in Chicago; first Olympic gold medalist to win a professional heavyweight title
Disney announces it has cast Chinese actress Liu Yifei to play Mulan in up-coming live action film
Billy Idol [William Broad], British-American pop-punk singer ("White Wedding"; "Dancing With Myself"), born in Stanmore, England
First international soccer game, Scotland draws with England (0-0) in Glasgow
The first German Beer Purity Law (Reinheitsgebot) is promulgated in Munich by Albert IV, Duke of Bavaria, stating beer should be brewed from only three ingredients – water, malt and hops
UNESCO adds Belgian beer to its Cultural Heritage List
London's Crystal Palace (built 1851) destroyed by fire
UNESCO announces new additions to its Intangible Cultural Heritage list, including the French Baguette, oral traditions of calling camels in the Middle East, and tea practices in China
NBA starKobe Bryant(LA Lakers) announces his intention to retire at the end of the season
Longtime "Jeopardy!" championKen Jenningsof Salt Lake City, Utah finally loses, leaving him with $2,520,700 USD, television's all-time biggest game show haul
Pink Floyd's album "The Wall" is released, sells 6 million copies in 2 weeks
Jackie Robinsonaccuses the NY Yankees of racial bias on national television
FIFA Women's World Cup Final, Tianhe Stadium, Guangzhou, China: Michelle Akers scores twice as US beats Norway, 2-1
7.0 magnitude earthquake near Anchorage, Alaska
Cyclone lashes Bangladesh, Eastern India; 317 killed
Pike Place Market dedicated in Seattle
First commercially successful AC electric power plant opens, Buffalo, NY
A German engineer patents front-wheel drive for automobiles
Police free kidnapped beer magnate Alfred Heineken in Amsterdam
First Beatles album in 25 years, "Live at the BBC," is released in Britain
Barbados becomes a Republic, removingQueen Elizabeth IIas head of state in a ceremony with Sandra Mason sworn in as the first President, withRihannadeclared a national hero
John Sentamu becomes the first black archbishop in the Church of England with his enthronement as the 97th Archbishop of York
Gordon Parks, American film pioneering black director, photographer and author (Learning Tree), born in Fort Scott, Kansas (d. 2006)
Colin Mochrie, Canadian improvisational comedian and producer (Whose Line is it Anyway?), born in Kilmarnock, Scotland
Gary Lineker, English soccer striker (80 caps; Leicester City, Everton, Tottenham Hotspur) and broadcaster (BBC, BT Sport, ITV), born in Leicester, England
Allan Sherman, American parody singer and songwriter ("Hello Muddah, Hello Faddah"), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 1973)
Robert Guillaume [Williams], American actor (Rafiki in "The Lion King"; Soap; Benson; Sports Night), born in St Louis, Missouri (d. 2017)
June Pointer, American pop and R&B singer (The Pointer Sisters - "Fire"; "He's So Shy"; "Jump (For My Love)'; "Slow Hand"), born in Oakland, California (d. 2006)

Paul Westphal, American Basketball Hall of Fame guard (NBA C'ship 1974; NBA All-Star 1977–81; Boston Celtics, Phoenix Suns) and coach (Phoenix Suns, Seattle SuperSonics, Sacramento Kings), born in Torrance, California (d. 2021)
Emmanuel Lubezki, Mexican cinematographer (Children of Men, Gravity), born in Mexico City
(Johnny) "Shuggie" Otis [Veliotes], American rock singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist ("Strawberry Letter 23"; "Inspiration Information"), born in Los Angeles California
Keith Giffen, American comic book writer and artist (Legion of Super-Heroes, Justice League), born in Queens, New York (d. 2023)
Britain signs agreement recognizing US independence
Barbados gains independence from Great Britain (National Day)
Rebellion of miners at Eureka Stockade at Ballarat in Victoria who swear allegiance to the Southern Cross Flag, angry at the colonial government - landmark event in Australian labor relations
King of DenmarkCnut the Great[Canute] claims the English throne after the death of Edmund Ironside
Battle at Narva: Swedish forces of KingCharles XIIdefeat Russian army [NS]
Vladimir K. Zworykinreceives patent on Iconoscope TV system
1960 NFL Draft:Billy Cannonfrom LSU first pick by Los Angeles Rams
Pope Francisurges peace while visiting controversial mosque in Bangui's PK5 district in Central African Republic
Australia experiences its hottest spring and second-hottest November recorded

Johnnie Jones, American attorney, civil rights activist (1953 Baton Rouge Bus Boycott), and US Army veteran (Normandy Invasion - Purple Heart; Battle of the Bulge), born in Laurel Hill, Louisiana (d. 2022)[1]

Emmy and Peabody Award-winning TV movie "Brian's Song", about the friendship of Chicago Bears football teammates Brian Piccolo andGale Sayers(based on Sayers' autobiography) premieres on ABC, starringJames Caanand Billie Dee Williams
Ina Garten's cooking TV show "Barefoot Contessa" premieres on the Food Network
RCA releases "Breakaway",Kelly Clarkson's 2nd studio album; wins 2 Grammy Awards and becomes her best selling album to date with over 12 million units sold
American folk artist "Grandma" Moses' 1943 painting "Sugaring Off" sells for a record $1.3 million for the artist, by Christie's in New York
1st indoor softball game in Chicago
First photo facsimile is transmitted across the Atlantic by radio (London to NYC)
Work begins on 1st US underwater highway tunnel in Chicago
